title: Streams, Redirection, Quoting, and Pipelines · System Forensics
summary: Build a repeatable incident-report pipeline whose data and diagnostics remain distinct and whose failures cannot be hidden by downstream success. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a stream-routing and pipeline-trace workbook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Trace standard input, standard output, standard error, file descriptors, and terminal attachment across a command.
Predict truncating, appending, input, descriptor duplication, and combined redirection from left-to-right order.
Apply unquoted, single-quoted, double-quoted, escaped, and ANSI-C quoted contexts to preserve intended arguments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — A 5-record incident command writes selected rows to report.txt and diagnostics to report.err. One grep stage finds no matches; predict descriptor routing and final status with pipefail before running. Competency probe: Trace standard input, standard output, standard error, file descriptors, and terminal attachment across a command.

Inspect the Stdin Stdout Stderr trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace standard input, standard output, standard error, file descriptors, and terminal attachment across a command.

content.text: For Stdin Stdout Stderr, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Stdin Stdout Stderr precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A 5-record incident command writes selected rows to report.txt and diagnostics to report.err. One grep stage finds no matches; predict descriptor routing and final status with pipefail before running. Competency probe: Trace standard input, standard output, standard error, file descriptors, and terminal attachment across a command.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: All command output travels through one stream.
